title: Ensure S3 bucket policy changes are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure S3 bucket policy changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for changes to S3 bucket policies.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring changes to S3 bucket policies may reduce the time it takes to detect and correct permissive policies on sensitive S3 buckets.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> § Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> § Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: 3. aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 4. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: 5. "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventSource = s3.amazonaws.com) && (($.eventName = PutBucketAcl) || ($.eventName = PutBucketPolicy) || ($.eventName = PutBucketCors) || ($.eventName = PutBucketLifecycle) || ($.eventName = PutBucketReplication) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketPolicy) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketCors) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketLifecycle) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketReplication)) }" 6. Note the <s3-bucket-policy-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 7. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <s3-bucket-policy-changes-metric> captured in step 4: 8. aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<s3-bucket-policy-changes-metric>]' 9. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 10. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: 11. aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for changes to S3 bucket policies and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: 2. aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name <s3-bucket-policy-changes-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=<s3-bucket-policy-changes-metric>,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventSource = s3.amazonaws.com) && (($.eventName = PutBucketAcl) || ($.eventName = PutBucketPolicy) || ($.eventName = PutBucketCors) || ($.eventName = PutBucketLifecycle) || ($.eventName = PutBucketReplication) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketPolicy) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketCors) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketLifecycle) || ($.eventName = DeleteBucketReplication)) }' Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 3. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: 4. aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 5. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: 6. aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 7. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: 8. aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <s3-bucket-policy-changes-alarm> --metric-name <s3-bucket-policy-changes-metric> --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.default_value: By default, CloudTrail logs S3 bucket policy changes (e.g., PutBucketPolicy, DeleteBucketPolicy), but no CloudWatch metric filters or alarms exist. These events are recorded but not actively monitored unless configured.
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control can be fully verified via AWS APIs and AWS CLI commands as indicated by the comprehensive audit and remediation sections. The required properties related to CloudTrail and CloudWatch for monitoring S3 bucket policy changes can be queried through available interfaces, making it appropriate for an automated audit approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_metric_filter_s3_bucket_policy_changes_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **AWS API Calls or boto3 SDK Methods:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail')` to call `describe_trails()` to list all active CloudTrail trails.
   - Use `get_trail_status()` to check if the specified trail is active and logging.
   - Use `get_event_selectors()` to ensure it includes management events.
   - Use `boto3.client('logs')` to call `describe_metric_filters()` to check for existing metric filters in the specified log group.
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch')` to call `describe_alarms()` to check for alarms related to the specified metric.
   - Use `boto3.client('sns')` to call `list_subscriptions_by_topic()` to ensure that the SNS topic has active subscribers.

2. **AWS CLI Commands:**
   - `aws cloudtrail describe-trails`
   - `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>`
   - `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>`
   - `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>`
   - `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<s3-bucket-policy-changes-metric>]'`
   - `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>`

3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - List all CloudTrail trails in the account.
   - Identify the specific log group associated with the multi-region CloudTrail trail.
   - Enumerate metric filters for the identified log group.
   - Check CloudWatch alarms related to specific metric filters.
   - Verify SNS subscriptions for the alarms.

4. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail configured.
   - Validate that the trail is logging and tracking management events.
   - Confirm that the metric filter pattern detects changes to S3 bucket policies as specified.
   - Ensure there’s at least one active alarm associated with the defined metric.
   - Verify that the SNS topic has active subscriptions.

5. **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - Pass: At least one multi-region CloudTrail trail is active (IsLogging=true), includes management events, has an appropriate metric filter set, and has a working SNS notification system.
   - Fail: If any of the checks for the CloudTrail trail activity, metric filter presence, alarm setup, or SNS subscription are missing or inactive.

6. **Example:**
   - Check if the metric filter with pattern '...putBucketPolicy...' exists for detected S3 bucket policy changes in CloudWatch Logs by executing the relevant boto3 calls or CLI commands as outlined above.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively verified using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI commands. The necessary data for auditing the monitoring of S3 bucket policy changes can be gathered programmatically through these interfaces, which aligns with AWS SDK capabilities and best practices for automation.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The automation details are mostly accurate. The boto3 methods and CLI commands listed are valid for checking CloudTrail configurations, CloudWatch metric filters, alarms, and SNS subscriptions.
- The program name `aws_cloudtrail_metric_filter_s3_bucket_policy_changes_monitoring` is descriptive and follows a logical naming convention, though it could be slightly more concise.
- There is a minor omission in ensuring that the metric filter pattern specifically captures S3 bucket policy changes. The example pattern '...putBucketPolicy...' should be validated for accuracy against actual CloudTrail event patterns.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

1. **Metric Filter Pattern Validation:**
   - Ensure that the metric filter pattern accurately captures all relevant API calls related to S3 bucket policy changes. The pattern should match events like `PutBucketPolicy`, `DeleteBucketPolicy`, and `GetBucketPolicy`.

2. **Program Name Suggestion:**
   - Consider a more concise program name: `aws_cloudtrail_s3_policy_change_monitoring`.

3. **Additional Checks:**
   - Verify that the CloudWatch Logs are correctly integrated with CloudTrail to capture the necessary logs.
   - Ensure that the SNS topic used for notifications is configured with the correct permissions and policies to allow CloudWatch to publish messages.

4. **Alternative AWS API Methods:**
   - Consider using `boto3.client('s3')` to directly verify bucket policies if needed for additional checks or validation.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured and technically sound, with minor improvements suggested for completeness and precision.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Keep as-is with minor modifications

- The approach is fundamentally correct, but I recommend incorporating the suggested improvements for a more robust audit process. Specifically, refine the metric filter pattern and consider the additional checks and program name refinement for clarity and precision.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the control can be effectively monitored using AWS APIs and CLI commands. The necessary data for auditing changes to S3 bucket policies can be gathered programmatically through AWS CloudTrail and CloudWatch Logs, which are accessible via boto3 and AWS CLI. This aligns with AWS best practices for automation, ensuring real-time monitoring and alerting.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** 
aws_cloudtrail_s3_policy_change_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, SNS
- **API/CLI Method:** 
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()`
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status()`
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors()`
  - `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters()`
  - `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()`
  - `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic()`
- **Properties to Check:** 
  - Ensure CloudTrail is multi-region and logging.
  - Verify metric filters in CloudWatch Logs for S3 bucket policy changes.
  - Check CloudWatch alarms are set for these metric filters.
  - Confirm SNS topic has active subscriptions for alerts.
- **Pass Criteria:** 
  - Active multi-region CloudTrail with logging enabled.
  - Correct metric filters and alarms configured for S3 bucket policy changes.
  - SNS topic with active subscribers for alerting.
- **Fail Criteria:** 
  - No active multi-region CloudTrail or logging disabled.
  - Missing or incorrect metric filters and alarms.
  - SNS topic without active subscribers.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no disagreements between the AI opinions; both supported an automated approach. The second opinion suggested a more concise program name, which has been adopted.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Amazon_Web_Services_Foundations_Benchmark_v6.0.0
